title: 그렇게 말하면 여자가 확 달라 보여요
text: Someone asked me, "I just want to know if you have a girlfriend." So, I guess I should go to a private detective agency. Then, I’d like to get hit with that just once. I keep thinking about that line I heard on a show a few episodes ago. Back then, a woman looked straight at a man from under an umbrella and said, " How about me?" I’ve actually thought about that a few times even after the show ended. I was drinking with a friend and thought, "Hey, that line is totally messed up, right?" No, seriously, just... ah, look at the heart. Let's say I didn't fall in love with this woman at first sight. I thought she was someone I could date, but since I have n't been thinking much about dating lately, I just stayed quiet. Then, suddenly, when she comes at me with that "How about me?" line, the guy gets a bit dazed and might just go all out. No, no, it’s not like she has to be stunningly beautiful. Because if she were stunningly beautiful, I would have already developed a crush on her. But it’s not like that. While I might have a top-tier ideal with outstanding looks that I want to meet, this is just a middle ground where I think, "I could handle this." But in that situation, if she suddenly hits me hard with a surprise, intense shot, her charm just skyrockets. I told you she's coming. Seriously, "My Wife Got Married" Son Ye-jin wearing only a raincoat and naked... are you trying to get on top of Son Ye-jin again? No, no, I do n't want to talk about it that way. Son Ye-jin... what, doing a handstand and just eating... men say they'll marry her. But it's not like that; I'm saying that in reality, that kind of confidence becomes the deciding factor. I think you really have to do it for it to be possible. Up to half a bottle of soju is fine. But if you're too nervous and eat a whole bottle, and then you start saying, "Drain it out more," and so on, then... ah, time... ah, sorry, I'll be going first. As your chin slides, "Uh, how do I look? How do I look?" Mom... Auntie, just bring me the chopsticks. Auntie, wow, this is going to be a beep. Wow, men have diverse tastes. And this is going to be a beep again. It might happen. I'm not like that. I think moderately flushed ears and cheeks are enough preparation.

title: 그렇게 말하면 여자가 확 달라 보여요
text: ust bring me the chopsticks. Auntie, wow, this is going to be a beep. Wow, men have diverse tastes. And this is going to be a beep again. It might happen. I'm not like that. I think moderately flushed ears and cheeks are enough preparation. Mulka in the soup, dangogo... how do I look? I think it'll be too salty. If you have that kind of courage output, courage creates good results in important moments. One day, I got drunk and meant to tell my best colleague, but it became an opportunity for my crush, and I ended up saying "I like you," and we started dating. I remember. There are times when you get a difficult urge and go crazy, but since you started anyway, that’s fine. You just say, "It’s like this, it’s like that," and you mark someone in your heart, but you don't end up dating them. You might say to a friend, "Isn't Jun-sik cute?" and they reply, "Jun-sik is here." Jun-sik is definitely cute. Anyway, I’ll buy it. Anyway, I’ll buy it. If that’s all that matters, right? You all have gone through many battlefields, and it seems that you don't always manage to capture the target you intended just like this. You know how we eat, drink, and hang out 2-on- 2 or 3-on-3? What happens if things fizzle out while drinking? Instead of going on a date with the person you like, you end up walking with someone whose house is in a similar direction. Earlier, I liked this friend over there, but their house is on the opposite side. Since I’m going this way now, I end up walking with the person going in the same direction. Then, you think, "Shall we have another drink?" or "Let's go just the two of us." As you drink, you realize, "Well, this person is kind of cute in their own way." If the wheel of fate turns in your direction tonight, it just rolls like that. It just happens, I guess. Then, you wake up the next day, and you just text and stuff. So you meet again to cherish her, and you start something like a "flirting" phase, and things like that. It's just... uh, I mean, what I'm saying is that things don't always go exactly the way you expect. Love is life, too. In those cases, Jun-sik usually pretends to not hear as much as possible.

title: 그렇게 말하면 여자가 확 달라 보여요
text: like a "flirting" phase, and things like that. It's just... uh, I mean, what I'm saying is that things don't always go exactly the way you expect. Love is life, too. In those cases, Jun-sik usually pretends to not hear as much as possible. He whispers, "You must have liked her since you started dating." The reason they ended up dating is because he had those feelings, to some extent. But that "some extent" doesn't mean he was head over heels for the girl. If he thinks she's worth seeing, he tries flirting, dates her once, and so on. He said he'd never heard such a bold confession before. It was beyond bold—it was n't like a Coupang delivery service, but just... straight up, Gu-gi-hyung. He just said, "Hey, you're totally hopeless." "My lower abdomen is tingling, right?" Distinguishing between being drunk and pretending to be drunk—I did n't really know when I was young, but I started to see it around my mid-to-late 20s. But if it's necessary, you fall for it even though you know it's true. The girl in front of you is pretending to be drunk, and if you feel that her being drunk can improve your situation, you just pretend to fall for it. " Ah, she's a drunk person right now." I guess they want to ask if it shows or not. When you're really drunk, you can't fix your appearance. You know what I mean, right? Like, a strand of hair gets caught on something, and your bangs stick out like this. Even when I look at myself pouring drinks, I ca n't do it well. But girls who pretend to be drunk

title: 그렇게 말하면 여자가 확 달라 보여요
text: u can't fix your appearance. You know what I mean, right? Like, a strand of hair gets caught on something, and your bangs stick out like this. Even when I look at myself pouring drinks, I ca n't do it well. But girls who pretend to be drunk use this kind of tone of voice to act drunk. Because women fundamentally have the attitude that they need to look pretty for the man in front of them, they can't act beyond a certain point. It's like those dramas where the heroine makes just this part red and says, "So, you and I are dating starting today," or something like that. You could say it lacks creativity, but since that kind of thing comes on so often whenever you turn on a drama, girls tend to do it that way too. The dividing line between pretending to be drunk and actually being drunk is that there is a limit to how much you can go down looking ugly. That is the last thing they just can't let go of. When you look at a woman in front of you and think, "Ah, she's drunk," and she wipes her cappuccino like this—" Yes, ah, ah, I guess, yes"—you think, "Wow, hey, hey, she must be really drunk. She must be really drunk." Even when girls pretend to be drunk, they don't act like that. When I come back from the bathroom, my lips and things like that are perfectly back in their original positions. They're just talking about being drunk. Unless I'm actually very drunk myself, it's not that hard to tell when a girl is faking it. And there's something really certain, more than anything else. If I say, "I'll be right back at 1 AM," and head to the bathroom, then say, "Oh, I left my phone behind," and come back, if I see the girl, she just walks up to me and says, "Oh, oh, I have n't left yet." Right? Really drunk girls are usually just waiting for me to come back from the bathroom, expecting something or something.

title: 그렇게 말하면 여자가 확 달라 보여요
text: my phone behind," and come back, if I see the girl, she just walks up to me and says, "Oh, oh, I have n't left yet." Right? Really drunk girls are usually just waiting for me to come back from the bathroom, expecting something or something. Do men ever pretend to be drunk? Men pretend not to be drunk. They think the opposite way. "Oh, I'm getting drunk, let's rest for a bit"—that might just be faking it. When they say they're going to play with fire, when we get to the front of the accommodation, what happens today shouldn't be your responsibility or mine. We were just called here tonight by the direction the wheel of fate calls. Rather than saying you specifically wanted to go or I specifically wanted to go, both of us want the feeling that we were dragged here by an invisible force. Then. Thinking "it can't be helped," I did n't even bother opening the app to see if a Kakao Taxi would work or not, but we were exchanging meaningless conversations like "Ah, I can't catch a taxi," "Ah, I can't get a taxi." If we really intended to take a taxi, I could have hailed one myself, right? I could have hailed one myself. But when I was like, "Hey, taxis aren't coming here," and someone said, " Oh yeah, wait a minute, I'll turn on the app," and so on— unintentionally—we had to go that far. From the moment we entered the front of the accommodation, there was a toothbrush. "What, a toothbrush?" And as soon as we passed the motel entrance, they sprayed a mist like some kind of Condition, instantly waking me up. Suddenly, "This is for the 3rd floor. Oh, thank you, thank you. Oh, sir, it's unmanned these days, so... oh, I see. Uh, I don't go there often these days, so I don't really [music] know."
